source.name: Codex distilled implementation recipe from user screenshots
source.license_notes: Original implementation notes generated for this personal knowledge base.

layout_rules: Use CSS variables for glass opacity, blur radius, rim color, and accent glow so the style can be themed consistently.
Build one reusable glass primitive, then vary radius, size, and accent color for buttons, inputs, cards, and modals.
Combine pseudo-elements for top-left gloss and bottom-right chromatic glint.
accessibility_notes: Provide @supports fallback when backdrop-filter is unavailable.
Respect prefers-reduced-transparency or provide a high-contrast variant.
Do not animate blur radius aggressively on low-end devices.
implementation_hints: Core CSS: background: linear-gradient(... rgba white/transparent ...); backdrop-filter: blur(22px) saturate(1.35); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.55); box-shadow: outer shadow plus inset white highlight plus inset dark lower edge.
Gloss layer: ::before with white translucent linear/radial gradient positioned top-left, opacity around .55, pointer-events none.
Caustic layer: ::after with cyan/magenta/orange radial gradients near corners, mix-blend-mode screen, low opacity.
Text: use strong contrast; avoid pale gray on pale glass.
Performance: limit backdrop-filter surfaces and avoid applying it to hundreds of table rows.

quality.notes: Implementation recipe distilled from the provided reference images.

retrieval.ai_summary: For web implementation of Liquid Glass, create reusable glass primitives using translucent gradients, backdrop-filter blur/saturate, rounded capsules, inner highlights, layered shadows, pseudo-element gloss, and chromatic caustic glints; add fallbacks and contrast safeguards.
